mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 5.0% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates discount rates on equity valuations. This compression of present value of future corporate earnings produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and recession risk, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of months following PAYEMS declines, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS reflects consumer spending strength and aggregate demand. Sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS signals robust economic activity that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS growth does not precede positive SPX returns in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) measures real economic output and capacity utilization. A decline in INDPRO signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or early contraction phase
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Spearman rank correlation < 0.08 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal labor market health and housing demand. When HOUST declines while UNRATE rises simultaneously, this interaction indicates deteriorating economic conditions that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging labor market stress
failure_condition: The joint signal (HOUST decline AND UNRATE rise) does not predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and the federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL growth exceeds FEDFUNDS rate, real rates are negative, signaling monetary accommodation that supports equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary easing or accommodative policy regime
failure_condition: The interaction signal (negative real rates) does not predict positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market slack that reduces wage pressure and inflation expectations. This regime shift produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices lower terminal rate expectations.
regime_hint: transition from tight labor market to slack labor market
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher when UNRATE > 4.5% versus UNRATE ≤ 4.5% (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure aggregate demand strength. When both GDPC1 and RSAFS show sustained growth, this interaction signals robust economic expansion that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with sustained demand growth
failure_condition: The joint signal (strong GDP and retail sales growth) does not predict positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in predictive regression.
